name: Improve AI support email drafts with Gmail, OpenAI and PostgreSQL

parameters.query: SELECT last_processed_sent_at FROM feedback_run_log WHERE status = 'completed' ORDER BY run_completed_at DESC LIMIT 1;
INSERT INTO feedback_run_log (run_started_at, status) VALUES (NOW(), 'running') RETURNING id;

SELECT id, gmail_thread_id, gmail_message_id, original_email_body, classification, ai_draft_text, feedback_processed_at, was_approved_as_is FROM ai_drafts WHERE gmail_thread_id = '{{ $json.threadId }}' LIMIT 1;
UPDATE ai_drafts SET was_approved_as_is = TRUE, feedback_processed_at = NOW() WHERE id = {{ $json.draftId }};

INSERT INTO corrections (
  ai_draft_id,
  original_email_body,
  classification,
  ai_draft_text,
  human_sent_text,
  diff_summary,
  embedding,
  source,
  kb_updated
) VALUES (
  {{ $json.draftId }},
  $1,
  $2,
  $3,
  $4,
  $5,
  $6::vector,
  'feedback_loop',
  FALSE
) RETURNING id;
UPDATE ai_drafts SET feedback_processed_at = NOW() WHERE id = {{ $('⚙️ Extract Embedding').item.json.draftId }};

nodes.name: 🗄️ DB - Fetch KB Entry to Update

parameters.query: SELECT id, category, question, answer FROM kb_data WHERE category = $1 ORDER BY updated_at DESC LIMIT 1;

values.content: You are updating a knowledge base entry for a customer support system at InCred Money, a platform for trading unlisted pre-IPO shares. Rewrite the KB answer to incorporate new information while preserving all existing correct content. Respond with ONLY the new answer text. No preamble, no explanation, no markdown.

parameters.query: UPDATE kb_data SET previous_answer = answer, answer = $1, updated_by = 'ai_feedback_loop', updated_at = NOW() WHERE id = {{ $('🗄️ DB - Fetch KB Entry to Update').item.json.id }};
UPDATE corrections SET kb_updated = TRUE WHERE ai_draft_id = {{ $('⚙️ Extract Embedding').item.json.draftId }} AND source = 'feedback_loop' ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT 1;
UPDATE feedback_run_log SET run_completed_at = NOW(), last_processed_sent_at = NOW(), status = 'completed' WHERE id = {{ $('🗄️ DB - Start Run Log').first().json.id }};

parameters.content:
## Self-learning feedback loop for AI email drafts

This workflow runs every 3 hours and checks which AI-generated 
email drafts have been reviewed and sent by the support team. 
It compares the AI draft against what was actually sent, learns 
from the differences, and stores human-approved responses as 
training examples for future drafts.

Over time, the main email workflow surfaces increasingly relevant 
past responses via vector similarity search — no fine-tuning needed.

## How it works

1. Fetches watermark from last completed run — only new sent 
   emails are processed each time
2. Gmail Sent folder is fetched since the watermark timestamp
3. Each sent email is matched to an AI draft via thread ID
4. Already-processed drafts are skipped automatically
5. AI compares the draft vs actual sent email and classifies 
   the type of edit made
6. If sent unchanged — marked approved-as-is, loop continues
7. If edited — embedding generated, correction saved to DB 
   for future similarity search
8. If edit reveals missing KB info — KB entry auto-updated

## Setup steps

1. Connect Gmail OAuth2 to the Gmail Fetch Sent node
2. Connect PostgreSQL credential to all DB nodes
3. Connect OpenAI API to the Chat Model and embedding nodes
4. Run the DB migration SQL to add feedback columns and 
   create the feedback_run_log table
5. Ensure Workflow 1 (email draft automation) is already 
   active and generating drafts
6. Activate — runs automatically every 3 hours

## Schedule & Fetch Emails

Runs every 3 hours. Fetches last_processed_sent_at from the 
most recent completed run as a watermark — so only new sent 
emails are fetched each time. First-ever run defaults to 7 days ago.

## Loop & thread matching

Processes one sent email at a time. Full message body is fetched 
via Gmail API separately — the Sent folder list only returns a 
snippet. Each email is matched to an AI draft by thread ID. 
No match or already processed = skip.

## AI comparison & branching

GPT-4o-mini compares the AI draft vs human-sent email and returns 
edit type, a plain-English diff summary, and whether a KB update 
is needed.

Approved as-is → mark draft, continue loop
Edited/rewritten → generate embedding, save to corrections table

## Corrections & KB update

Human-edited pairs are embedded and saved to the corrections 
table — this is what Workflow 1 queries for similarity search 
when drafting future replies.

If the edit contained new information, the matching KB entry 
is rewritten by AI. Previous answer is preserved for audit.

main.node: 🗄️ DB - Fetch KB Entry to Update
